I'll search for the Gurobi Optimizer Reference Manual to verify this reference.
Based on my search results, I have found the Gurobi Optimizer Reference Manual as a real, official technical documentation source.

The search confirms that:
1. The official documentation site (docs.gurobi.com) hosts the "Gurobi Optimizer Reference Manual"
2. Multiple versions exist (Version 10.0, Version 13.0, and the current version)
3. The documentation is published by Gurobi Optimization, LLC
4. The cited URL (https://www.gurobi.com) is the main company website, which directs users to the official documentation

This is a valid technical documentation/reference manual from an official software vendor, which falls under the category of non-academic real-world sources (specifically, official vendor technical documentation). Such references are valid citations even though they are not academic papers and do not appear in academic databases like Semantic Scholar or CrossRef.

EXPLANATION: The Gurobi Optimizer Reference Manual is authentic official vendor documentation published by Gurobi Optimization, LLC on their documentation portal; non-academic technical references are valid citations.
LINK: https://docs.gurobi.com/projects/optimizer/en/current/index.html
FOUND_TITLE: Gurobi Optimizer Reference Manual
FOUND_AUTHORS: Gurobi Optimization, LLC
FOUND_YEAR: 2024
VERDICT: UNLIKELY
